signal.title: AI drives semiconductor market to US$1.8 trillion by 2030, China leads capacity and equipment

signal.title: The latest in data centers, AI, and energy

These are highly relevant and interconnected topics for sophisticated investors and technologists, as they underpin chip development, startup viability, and funding trends, providing valuable macro-level insight.

signal.title: iyO amends lawsuit against OpenAI’s io, now alleges trade secret theft
signal.reason: The headline signals a serious legal dispute against OpenAI involving 'trade secret theft,' a critical allegation directly impacting IP, valuation, and competitive dynamics for AI models, chips, startups, and funding. This is high-signal information for sophisticated investors and technologists.

macro_geo.headline: Fed Faces Inflation Pressure as CPI Hits 327.46
macro_geo.insight: With CPI at 327.46 and Fed Funds at 3.64%, the Fed is likely to maintain or hike rates to curb persistent inflation, risking slowdown in growth; VIX at 31.1 signals market unease over policy tightening.

macro_geo.headline: US-China Tariff Tensions Escalate
macro_geo.insight: Renewed US tariffs on Chinese tech and manufacturing goods expected in Q2 2026, escalating trade friction; China may retaliate with rare earth export curbs, impacting global supply chains and tech stocks.

macro_geo.headline: Energy Markets Volatile Amid Geopolitical Risks
macro_geo.insight: Oil prices spike to $95/barrel on Middle East supply concerns and OPEC+ production cuts; high energy costs could fuel inflation further, while renewable sectors in Europe gain traction with policy support.

regulation.headline: EU AI Act Finalized with Stricter Compliance Deadlines
regulation.insight: The EU AI Act, fully ratified in late 2025, has accelerated compliance deadlines for high-risk AI systems to mid-2026, impacting US and Chinese tech firms operating in the EU. Penalties for non-compliance can reach up to 7% of global annual turnover, forcing companies to prioritize transparency and risk assessment or face market exclusion.

regulation.headline: US Congress Introduces Bipartisan Crypto Regulation Bill
regulation.insight: On March 25, 2026, US Congress proposed a landmark bill to classify most cryptocurrencies as commodities under CFTC oversight, sidelining SEC's security-based approach. This could streamline market operations for crypto firms but faces uncertainty with potential presidential veto and ongoing antitrust concerns over market concentration in crypto exchanges.

hot_takes.take: AI is overhyped in 2026—everyone’s chasing ‘AGI’ like it’s the second coming, but we’re stuck with glorified chatbots that still can’t reason beyond pattern matching. The real value is in narrow, industry-specific AI, and most startups are bleeding cash on pipe dreams. Stop buying the hype.

Markets are sleepwalking into a 2026 bubble—tech valuations are absurd, fueled by cheap debt and FOMO. The Fed’s dovish pivot is a trap; inflation isn’t dead, and rate hikes will return by Q4, popping this house of cards. Cash is king for now.

Crypto is back, baby—Bitcoin’s push past $100K isn’t a fluke, it’s institutional adoption finally hitting critical mass. Regs are clarifying, ETFs are exploding, and DeFi is eating TradFi’s lunch. Ignore the 2022 PTSD; this is the cycle to go long.

Macro outlook screams stagflation—global growth is stalling with supply chain scars still fresh, while commodity prices keep creeping up. Central banks are paralyzed, too scared to tighten or ease. Equities will flatline, and ‘safe’ bonds are a joke. Look to real assets.
